rulings.Case Title: DOE B.M. ET AL VS. WESTIN ST. FRANCIS UNION SQUARE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order To Compel An Undertaking By Plaintiffs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 2, 2018, Line 2, DEFENDANT STARWOOD HOTELS AND RESORTS WORLDWIDE, INC.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order To Compel An Undertaking By Plaintiffs. Defendant Starwood Hotel and Resorts Worldwide, Inc's motion for an order to compel an undertaking by plaintiffs is DENIED. Starwood fails to show "that there is no reasonable possibility plaintiff[s] will prevail." (See Yao v. Sup. Ct. (2002) 104 Cal.App.4th 327, 333.) Indeed, Starwood has already lost an attempt to summarily adjudicate plaintiffs' cause of action for negligence and negligent supervision. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4 pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: SHERI XU VS. FIBROGEN, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Return This Case To The Civil Active List Based On Defendants' Waiver Of Right To Arbitrate By Refusal To Pay Arbitration Fees And/Or Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 2, 2018, Line 4, PLAINTIFF SHERI XU'S Notice Of Motion To Return This Case To The Civil Active List Based On Defendants' Waiver Of Right To Arbitrate By Refusal To Pay Arbitration Fees And/Or Costs. The motion is DENIED. Having ordered this matter to arbitration, the Court now has only "vestigial jurisdiction." (Titan/Value Equities Group, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1994) 29 Cal.App.4th 482, 487.) Not within that jurisdiction are disputes over filing fees, which are for the arbitrator to decide. (See id. at 487-88.) In any event, defendants have now paid the full arbitration filing fee, so the motion is moot. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4 pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: DOUGLAS D COLES ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To (1) Compel Plaintiff Erlinda T. Ritz To Provide Written Discovery Responses; And (2) Deem Requests For Admissions Set One Served Upon Plaintiff Erlinda T. Ritz As Admitted
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Wednesday, May 2, 2018, Line 1, Defendant Regents Of The University Of California's Motion To (1) Compel Plaintiff Erlinda T. Ritz To Provide Written Discovery Responses; And (2) Deem Requests For Admissions Set One Served Upon Plaintiff Erlinda T. Ritz As Admitted. Pro Tem Judge Roger Mead, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant Regents' unopposed motion is granted. Within 14 days of service of notice of entry of this order, Plaintiff Erlinda T. Ritz is directed to serve code-compliant responses, without objections, to Defendant Regents' Form Interrogatories (Set One), Special Interrogatories (Set One) and Request for Production of Documents (Set One), served on February 21, 2017. Plaintiff Erlinda T. Ritz is deemed to have admitted each of Defendant Regents' Requests for Admissions (Set One) served on May 16, 2017. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rmead@folgerlevin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH GREEN, JR. VS. IGMIDIO VINO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT .
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 2, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO MUSEUM OF MODERN ART'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant San Francisco Museum of Modern Art's motion for summary judgment is GRANTED. The only reasonable conclusion from the evidence is that SFMOMA hired Cyprus Security as an independent contractor and no employment relationship existed between SFMOMA and Cypress' security guards. "[T]he common law test for employment is a question of fact, while the legal conclusion to be drawn from those facts is a question of law. Even if one or two of the individual factors might suggest an employment relationship, summary judgment is nevertheless proper when, as here, all the factors weighed and considered as a whole establish that [Cypress] was an independent contractor." (See Arnold v. Mutual of Omaha Ins. Co. (2011) 202 Cal.App.4th 580, 590.) That SFMOMA retained a right to exclude guards from its museum or require their reassignment, and approved guard uniforms and Cypress' management plan, is insufficient to establish an employment relationship. Under the Cypress-SFMOMA contract, Cypress retained control and discretion over its employees and performance of their work. (Agmt., Ex. 1, pp.2, 3.) SFMOMA did not supervise Cypress guards, pay them directly, hire them or set their schedules. (Varah Dec.) Also, Cypress engages in a distinct occupation requiring special skills, the contract is for a two-year term, and the parties' clear intent was to create a hirer-independent contractor relationship. (See Agmt., Ex. 1, p. 3.) Thus, SFMOMA cannot be vicariously liable for torts of independent contractor Cyprus Security nor directly liable under a theory of negligent hiring. (See Camargo v. Tjaarda Dairy (2001) 25 Cal.4th 1235, 1238-39). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4 pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for SFMOMA is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH GREEN, JR. VS. IGMIDIO VINO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 2, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANT CYPRESS SECURITY, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant Cypress Security, LLC's motion for summary judgment is GRANTED. Kenneth Green's claims against Cyprus are barred by the exclusivity rule of worker's compensation law. (Lab. Code §3601(b).) The exception for assaults by an employer (§3602(1)) is not applicable. The undisputed evidence shows that Cypress did not ratify the acts of Igmidio Vino, a co-employee with no supervisorial authority, but rather banned Vino from the job site and then fired him. (Savchenko Dec. 7-9.) No ground exists for continuing the hearing. Trial is set for June 4 and, contrary to Green's representation, Vino appeared in this case 15 months ago; he could have been deposed long before now. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4 pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Cypress Security is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER DALLING VS. FCA US, LLA A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendants Person Most Knowledgeable And Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Wednesday, May 2, 2018, Line 2, Plaintiff Jennifer Dalling's Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendants Person Most Knowledgeable And Request For Production Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge Roger Mead, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to compel the production of a PMQ for deposition is granted in part and denied in part as follows: granted with respect to topics 1-4, 28 and 29 listed in Exhibit A to the Deposition Notice dated July 26, 2017; granted with respect to topic 5, revised to delete the phrase "and other REPAIR DOCUMENTS"; granted with respect to topics 23 and 24, unless the parties agree on procedures to use testimony on these topics provided in earlier cases; granted with respect to topic 25, limited to any third party dispute resolution program applicable to Plaintiff's complaints regarding the SUBJECT VEHICLE; granted with respect to topics 26 and 27, revised to read "Questions relating to the processes in place since January 1, 2017 to evaluate customer requests to have vehicles repurchased pursuant to California Civil Code Section 1793.2"; and denied as to topics 6-21 because the proposed definitions of the terms "ENGINE DEFECTS" and SUSPENSION DEFECTS" are overbroad and uncertain. With respect to those requests for production of documents at the deposition in Exhibit B to the Deposition Notice that are the subject of the motion, the motion is granted (to the extent the responsive documents have not already been produced) with respect to category 4; category 9, limited to written materials provided to authorized dealership technicians for use in diagnosing and repairing vehicles of the same make, model and vehicle year as the SUBJECT VEHICLE; and category 48, limited to documents in use since January 1, 2017; and denied with respect to categories 10, 14-19, 21, 24, 28, 31-36, 38 and 41. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rmead@folgerlevin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: 601 FOURTH STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery Requests By Defendant City And County Of San Francisco, To Compel Responses By Defendant San Francisco Municipal Transportation Agency, And To Have Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Wednesday, May 2, 2018, Line 3, Plaintiff 601 Fourth Street Owners Association's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery Requests By Defendant City And County Of San Francisco, To Compel Responses By Defendant San Francisco Municipal Transportation Agency, And To Have Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted. Pro Tem Judge Roger Mead, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant City and County of San Francisco's RFP responses do not include adequate statements of compliance (CCP 2031.210(a), 2031.220) and do not identify with particularity any documents or ESI covered by the objections (CCP 2031.240(b)(1)). CCSF's objections other than those based on a claim of privilege are overruled. With respect to any responsive documents withheld from production based on a claim of privilege, CCSF shall provide sufficient factual information for the other parties to evaluate the merits of the claim (CCP 2031.240(c)(1)). CCSF shall serve amended code-complaint responses to RFPs 1, 4, 5, 6, 13, 14, 17, 19, 22, 24, 29 and 32 within 14 days, and complete the production of documents in accordance with its statements of compliance therein within 28 days, of notice of entry of this order. CCSF's Response to Form Interrogatories 12.4, 12.7, 15.1 and 17.1 are deficient. Within 14 days of notice of entry of this order CCSF shall serve amended code complaint responses that shall be as complete and straightforward as the information reasonably available to CCSF permits (CCP 2030.220). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rmead@folgerlevin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling.
